Standard devtools loop: Efficiency (suite takes minutes — don’t re-run needlessly). check() already runs full test suite examples rebuilds vignettes (~3 min: ~90s tests + ~74s vignettes + ~20s examples), covr::package_coverage() runs suite — test() → check() → coverage() one gate executes suite ~3×. Instead: iterate targeted devtools::test(filter = "<x>") / testthat::test_file(); run failing tests way shows details (capture res <- use non-silent reporter — never silent--rerun); skip vignette rebuild mid-work checks check(vignettes = FALSE); final gate run check() (subsumes test()) coverage() . Never run two package-touching R processes concurrently. Scaffolding helpers: usethis::use_r(), use_test(), use_package(), use_testthat(3), use_github_action("check-standard"). Use testthat 3e, roxygen2 exported functions (document default, runnable @examples, @seealso cross-links).

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: scoring scheme scores linear map standardized observed variables, S = Z W, -level score correlations exactly: scores need materialized — R weight matrices. Waller (2007) derived orthogonal components, identity holds linear W, includes regression (Thurstone, W = R^{-1} Λ), Bartlett, tenBerge scores. standard PCA EFA/ESEM (regression-scored) paths algebra-eligible. Standardization trap. Component/factor scores unit-variance general (Bartlett oblique scores especially). Always divide real score SDs sqrt(diag(W'RW)). Assuming unit variance silently corrupts correlations.
code: cor(S_a, S_b) = D_a^{-1/2} (W_a' R W_b) D_b^{-1/2}            where  R   = input correlation matrix (pearson or polychoric)                   D_x = diag(W_x' R W_x)   # score variances; NOT assumed 1

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Two situations force scores route: 1. Nonlinear scoring — EAP/MAP/ML scores categorical/ordinal ESEM. (ordinal ESEM instead uses regression scores polychoric loadings, stays linear → algebra polychoric R.) 2. User wants empirical, sample-realized correlations (including FIML/missing-data realities) rather model-implied quantity. missingness differ; document one “edge” represents (default: model-consistent / algebra eligible).
code: compute_edges(levels, R,               edge_method = c("auto", "algebra", "scores"),               pairs  = c("adjacent", "all"),   # "all" for Forbes extension               data   = NULL,               align  = TRUE,               use    = "pairwise") {    for (each pair (a, b) in chosen pairs) {      algebra_ok <- levels[[a]]$scoring$linear &&                   levels[[b]]$scoring$linear && !is.null(R)      if (edge_method == "algebra" || (edge_method == "auto" && algebra_ok)) {       Wa <- levels[[a]]$scoring$weights       Wb <- levels[[b]]$scoring$weights       C  <- crossprod(Wa, R %*% Wb)                       # W_a' R W_b       sa <- sqrt(diag(crossprod(Wa, R %*% Wa)))           # score SDs       sb <- sqrt(diag(crossprod(Wb, R %*% Wb)))       E  <- sweep(sweep(C, 1, sa, "/"), 2, sb, "/")       # standardize     } else {       stopifnot(!is.null(data))                           # nonlinear/EAP or edge_method="scores"       Sa <- score(levels[[a]], data); Sb <- score(levels[[b]], data)       E  <- cor(Sa, Sb, use = use)     }      if (align) E <- align_signs(E, lineage)               # see §7     edges[[pair]] <- E   }   # return: list of (k_a x k_b) matrices  +  tidy edge tibble (from, to, r, is_primary, above_cut) }

text: IDs: models numbered factor count (m1…mK); factors within model m{k}f{j}. Deterministic within-level order = primary-parent (recursive), variance tiebreak. Sort factors within level (primary parent’s position level , descending variance). single top factor ties fall back variance. places child parent splits read cleanly; f{j} ID order follows layout order tables plots agree. Limitation: ordering reduces can’t eliminate crossings (child strong secondary parent elsewhere still throws crossing edge); layout’s barycenter step (§11) refines x-position continuously beyond discrete order. Lineage lives edges/lineage, never ID. child can multiple strong parents (point method), don’t encode parentage m{k}f{j} — breaks structure isn’t clean tree. Matching: assign factor primary parent via greedy per-column argmax |r| (.e. apply(abs(E), 2, .max)). Multiple children sharing parent normal expected — adjacent levels always n_b = n_a + 1 (level k k factors vs k−1 ), pigeonhole least two children share parent. bijection (LSAP/Hungarian) therefore ill-posed removed. Tucker’s φ serves supplementary congruence criterion redundancy_phi filtering (Forbes extension, §14.19) used matching . Sign alignment — anchor primary parent, “positive.” Sign one DoF per factor; make every cross-level correlation positive. Rule: anchor m1f1 defined orientation (positive sum loadings, substantive marker item), orient factor correlation primary parent positive, propagating top-. Document non-primary edges may legitimately negative. Implementation note (M35): “propagating top-” means child’s flip chosen parent’s already-aligned sign, parent’s raw orientation — otherwise flipped parent leaves primary edge displaying negative. every primary-parent edge non-negative; secondary edges may red.

text: suggest_k() returns several complementary criteria consensus range, never single number. five criteria implemented (M12): Selective computation (M25). criteria argument (rlang::arg_match(multiple = TRUE), default five) lets callers request subset c("pa_pc", "pa_fa", "map", "vss", "cd"). Skipping real compute saving, output filtering: pa_pc/pa_fa share one fa.parallel(fa = "") call (neither run) map/vss share one vss() call, e.g. criteria = "map" avoids PA simulation entirely; vss toggles VSS-1+VSS-2 unit. Non-requested k_* fields NA; criteria data frame keeps stable schema (NA-filled non-run columns); object records criteria_requested. print()/autoplot() render requested criteria consensus range computed requested criteria . Empirical Kaiser Criterion (EKC) EGA (EGAnet) scope — additional deps without sufficient incremental benefit five criteria . Note: suggest_k() accepts cor = "pearson" (default) "spearman" ; "polychoric" supported (PA VSS polychoric eigen-decomposition path). Users analysing ordinal data run suggest_k() Pearson matrix switch cor = "polychoric" ackwards(). Report k maximum depth; users often deliberately set level two past consensus watch factors fragment. Note overextraction/non-replicability caution (Forbes 2023). Add seed argument reproducibility CD step — psych::fa.parallel() respond reliably set.seed(), PA simulation results vary across calls regardless seed. See vignette("ackwards-suggest-k") narrative/educational treatment five criteria including pros/cons, bias direction, engine--criterion pairing (M14).

States “series linked solutions, fitted hierarchy” caveat . summary.ackwards — per-level variance explained fit indices (ESEM); readable lineage list (m1f1 → m2f1, m2f2 → …); flagged redundant/artifact components object pruned via prune(). top_items(x, level, cut, n, sort, , show_labels) (M18; /show_labels M36) — salient-item listing, filtered |loading| >= cut sorted descending. Returns top_items S3 object grouped cli print method. = "factor" (default) groups items factor; = "item" inverts list, per item, factors loads (cross-loading view); n/sort apply within chosen unit. fit data carried per-column "label" attributes (captured meta$item_labels), show_labels = TRUE (default) prints items label (code) per-item bare-code fallback. title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Resolved (design round): 1. Rotation → varimax (orthogonal) across engines, hardcoded internal constant since M13; oblique rotation scope (resolved 2026-06 — confounds cross-level signal method’s whole point). rotation removed user argument M13. 2. Correlation basis → pearson default, polychoric opt-, ordinal-detection cli warning (silent switching). 3. Within-level order → primary-parent (recursive), variance tiebreak; f{j} IDs follow . 4. Ordinal ESEM scoring → tenBerge polychoric (linear → algebra) default; EAP opt-. Default Likert path need data edge time. 5. Package name → ackwards (verify via available::available("ackwards")). Resolved build (M1–M3): 6. Ordinal-detection heuristic: ≤ 7 distinct integer values triggers warning. 7. CF kappa: κ = 1/p (varimax-equivalent; Crawford & Ferguson 1970). kappa argument accepted stored never wired engine — engines hardcoded cfT → "varimax". Removed entirely M13: CF(κ=1/p) ≡ varimax; literature never varies kappa; exposing implied quartimax/equamax reasonable alternatives (method). 8. cor = "spearman" added alongside "pearson" non-polychoric rank-based option. 9. fm argument exposed EFA engine ("minres" default, "ml", "pa"). 10. cut_show argument exposed (default 0.3) control edges flagged above_cut. Resolved M4: 11. ESEM engine → lavaan::efa() (EFA--SEM, full ESEM structural paths). Gives per-level fit indices + rotation-aware SEs + WLSMV ordinal estimation. require full ESEM block syntax; bass-ackwards levels independent EFA solutions. 12. ESEM scoring → self-compute tenBerge weights lavaan’s estimated loadings Λ latent correlation matrix R via existing .tenBerge_weights(R, Λ). lavPredict() used default path (lacks tenBerge); eventual hook EAP opt-. 13. EAP scoring → scope (declined M28). EAP’s shrinkage attenuates cross-level correlations — primary signal bass-ackwards measures — making theoretically inferior tenBerge method. EAP request still returns cli_abort("yet implemented"). scores-route seam compute_edges() preserved, implementing EAP planned. 14. cor = "polychoric" → general basis engines (ESEM-). PCA/EFA compute R via psych::polychoric() ackwards() feed engine usual. ESEM uses lavaan’s latent correlation matrix R edges (mix psych-polychoric R lavaan-WLSMV loadings). 15. ESEM ordinal estimator → WLSMV default; ULSMV documented option. 16. loadings_se → added §4 level contract (p×k matrix, NULL PCA/EFA). Completed M4 (addition items 11–16 ): - NPD polychoric matrices: warn + smooth via psych::cor.smooth() (implemented). - Convergence truncation tested ESEM engine (k=4+ p=6 triggers lavaan error → warn + truncate → object builds k_eff=3). - Algebra-vs-scores cross-check documented Pearson/continuous paths ; polychoric ESEM edges (algebra uses lavaan polychoric R; scores route uses Pearson standardization) diverge design excluded oracle. Resolved M5 (Forbes extension): 17. API → two orthogonal args: pairs = c("adjacent","") prune = c("none","redundant","artefact") (char vector; default "none"). prune != "none" auto-upgrades pairs "" loud cli_inform() (chains need -levels edges assess). Superseded M34 (item 27 ): pruning now standalone prune() verb, ackwards() argument, pairs longer auto-upgrades. 18. Prune action → flag-, never remove. Adds pruned/prune_reason annotation columns edge/node tidy structures; object retains levels (preserves Invariant 5 algebra-vs-scores oracle). Pruning interpretive relabeling, re-estimation — say print/docs (extends §2 honesty caveat). 19. Redundancy → faithful Forbes (2023): score-correlation chains |r| ≥ .9 (default, tunable), retention rule = keep bottom node chain reaches level k (-specific, best-defined), else keep topmost node (broadest manifestation). Tucker’s φ (> .95, Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge 2006) computed aligned loadings optional conjunctive criterion. φ formula Σaᵢbᵢ / sqrt(Σaᵢ² · Σbᵢ²); base R, new dependency. 20. Additive enrichments paper (default output still matches Forbes’s examples): always report r φ every redundancy candidate (report-first, flag-second — borderline cases like paper’s .89/.93 alcohol component stay visible); report endpoint r (direct, -levels edges) alongside chain flag disagree (correlation non-transitive: clean adjacent chain neither implies implied endpoint identity — chain answers “perpetuates every level,” endpoint r answers “construct”). 21. Artifact → never auto-flagged. prune(x, "artifact") surfaces φ inspection; removal documented researcher judgment (Forbes explicit introduces researcher DoF / confirmation bias; cf. Wicherts et al. 2016). Resolved M32 (API-shape & naming; owner-reviewed, equivalent guidance elsewhere document): 22. tidy(x, = "fit") long-format key column → statistic, replacing index (read row position; actually held fit-index names EFA/ESEM eigenvalue positions PCA). Wide format (format = "wide") unaffected — column names still come values key. 23. k_max naming collision (ackwards() = extraction depth vs. suggest_k() = max factors/components evaluated) → keep shared name functions. ’re genuinely dial applied different workflow stages (suggest_k(k_max = ...) → ackwards(k_max = ...)); renaming one create vocabulary drift without removing real ambiguity. Resolved instead via roxygen: function’s @param k_max states meaning cross-references ’s. 24. tidy(= "fit") cutoff flags → removed. cutoffs = TRUE argument meets/{statistic}_meets columns produced (.flag_fit()) dropped; pass/fail boolean quietly endorsed Hu & Bentler (1999) thresholds package elsewhere (§9, section) treats conventional contested — continuing M28/M31 output-honesty trajectory. .fit_cutoffs() retained internal helper: autoplot(= "fit") still draws threshold reference lines summary() still annotates inline check/cross mark: visual guides, returned pass/fail column user mistake computed judgment. 25. Variance scale → proportion, 0-1 (tidy(= "variance") columns renamed variance_pct/cumulative_pct → proportion/cumulative, values divided 100). aligns tidy() engine’s internal variance slot (already 0-1; see print.ackwards, reads directly) broom/psych convention (psych reports “Proportion Var” 0-1). Percent formatting moves display layer (print(), summary(), vignette gt tables) rather living tidy data. 26. M31-deferred: effective ESEM estimator recorded $meta. M31 explicitly deferred (“better bundled M32’s meta/column decisions bolted ”): x$meta$estimator now stores effective estimator auto-selection ("ML"/"MLR"/"WLSMV"/"ULSMV"; NA PCA/EFA). summary() gains one-line footnote naming lavaan’s scaled-variant reporting (§14.M31 point 2/Post-review) whenever effective estimator "WLSMV"/"ULSMV"/"MLR" — shown "ML", scaled variant. five M32 changes breaking deprecation path (pre-CRAN, users; consistent M34 pruning-verb precedent clean moves compatibility shims). Resolved M34 (pruning verb; owner-reviewed, breaking, deprecation path — pre-CRAN, users): 27. Pruning extracted standalone, pipeable S3 generic prune() (prune.ackwards), ackwards() argument. five prune-related args (prune → renamed rules, redundancy_r, redundancy_phi, min_items, orphan_r) leave ackwards() entirely: ackwards(...) |> prune(...). Rationale: extraction expensive, deterministic step; pruning cheap interpretive. Separating lets researcher re-prune new thresholds without re-extracting (re-running ESEM per level expensive path M26 optimized). prune() generic (UseMethod), plain function, coexists prune generics already defined recursive-partitioning packages (e.g. rpart::prune) regardless package load order. Returns ackwards object $prune populated (replacing prior pruning) — new class, print/summary/tidy/glance/augment/ autoplot work unchanged. 28. Edges redundancy chains artifact φ recomputed fresh inside prune() via compute_edges(pairs = "") object’s stored levels/r (Invariant 3), never written back x$edges (Invariant 1: one edge path). ackwards()‘s pairs auto-upgrade "" pruning requested (M5 item 17) removed along — pairs now pure display/storage setting ackwards(), decoupled pruning; prune() works correctly regardless fit-time pairs value. 29. Manual pruning: prune(x, rules = "none", manual = c("m4f3", "m4f4")) flags user-named nodes directly (standalone, auto rule needed), unions onto auto rule’s flags (prune(x, "redundant", manual = c(...))). Unknown labels error. overlap auto rule manual, auto rule’s prune_reason wins (informative); otherwise-unflagged manual nodes get prune_reason = "manual". 30. Naming: canonical rule name "artifact" (US spelling, owner’s preference), "artefact" accepted alias (nod Commonwealth spelling Forbes’ usage) normalized internally "artifact". Existing code passing "artefact" keeps working. "tucker" considered rejected alias/rename: mode surfaces Tucker’s φ (also computes few_items/orphan/split_merge structural signals), naming statistic mislabel umbrella — "artifact" names mode . 31. ackwards() explicitly rejects five removed args passed via ... (rather silently absorbing ) pointer prune() — silent absorption masked-argument footgun, clean break intended (Invariant 6: loud, silent). Resolved M38 (missing = "fiml" PCA/EFA; reverses resolved default — owner-signed-): 32. FIML promoted first-class PCA/EFA route. M16 contract “missing = "fiml" errors PCA, EFA” reversed Pearson basis: engine = "pca"/"efa" cor = "pearson", missing = "fiml" now routes R psych::corFiml() (full-information ML, MVN) feeds existing W'RW algebra — Invariant-1-clean (one edge path, one corFiml call per run since R computed reused across levels; new dependency, psych already Imports). still errors non-Pearson PCA/EFA basis (Spearman, polychoric — corFiml honor ) WLSMV/ULSMV. .resolve_missing() gained cor argument enforce guard matrix. Grew M37 doc-planning observation user already smuggle FIML via M22 correlation-matrix seam (ackwards(corFiml(x), …)); M38 makes capability discoverable owns n_obs tradeoff. 33. n_obs string option + default. raw-data FIML PCA/EFA path, n_obs accepts "total" (default) "complete". "total" = every row contributing FIML likelihood, matching convention genuine FIML analysis reports (Enders 2010) giving cross-engine parallelism ESEM-ML FIML. Point estimates (loadings/edges) depend choice; EFA fit indices , approximate two-step (FIML matrix → normal-theory EFA) procedure regardless N (Zhang & Savalei 2020) — route announces caveat via cli (Invariant 6). "effective" considered dropped: canonical formula exists corFiml→EFA route, package-invented convention masquerading standard. string n_obs rejected path; cor-matrix input still requires numeric N. Known limitations / deferred future milestones: - factor_cor ESEM engine permuted variance-sort ord vector. Safe permanently: orthogonal rotation supported (factor_cor = ; permutation ), oblique rotation scope (§9, §14.1). guard comment engine_esem.R documents required decision ever reversed. - Algebra-vs-scores cross-check cover cor = "polychoric" paths (see ), missing = "fiml" PCA/EFA path (M38): algebra uses psych::corFiml() matrix scores route standardizes raw, NA-bearing data (pairwise Pearson SDs), two bases diverge missingness design — reason polychoric excluded. oracle tests therefore run complete-data linear engines; FIML/polychoric object fed , false-failure risk, cross-check certify paths. - cor = "spearman" + engine = "esem" semantically inconsistent (lavaan fits Pearson ML raw data edges use Spearman R); warning now emitted (M10). - ESEM engine detect warn improper/Heywood solutions — resolved M10: engine now inspects lavaan::lavInspect(fit, "theta") warns (diag(theta) <= 0) (parity EFA engine, Invariant 7). - ESEM ML/MLR missing = "pairwise": lavaan uses listwise deletion model fit edges computed separately-computed pairwise stats::cor() — minor inconsistency (fit statistics complete-case N, edges full pairwise N). Documented $meta$missing; per-call advisory warning fires whenever NAs detected. Use missing = "listwise" "fiml" resolve. (Added M16; §9 missing row cross-references .) - Forbes-extension improvements deferred past M5 (published method weaker spots worth strengthening later; M5 ships faithful method + §14.20 reporting enrichments): - Structural artefact signals. Done M25 (Wave 2). prune = "artefact" now populates x$prune$structural per-factor few_items / orphan / split_merge signals (flag/report ; auto-pruning). Args: min_items = 3L, orphan_r = 0.5. split_merge TRUE factor’s primary items came multiple different primary factors preceding level. CLI print()/summary() report flagged count. - Factor-score-indeterminacy caveat EFA/ESEM redundancy. Done M25 (Wave 3). redundancy_phi = NULL now auto-resolves: PCA → φ filter; EFA/ESEM → 0.95. NA explicit opt-. Announces via cli auto-applied (Invariant 6). See §9 defaults table. - Selection bias “strongest” edge. Plotting max correlation across many -levels pairs capitalizes chance (85 → 1,320 correlations levels grow). Add bootstrap CIs / SEs edges (reuse loadings_se infrastructure) strongest-edge claim inferentially honest. Deferred; warrants milestone (perf-heavy: resample re-runs full extraction every level; needs future parallelisation + print/plot/vignette integration). - M40 spin-(code/viz deferred doc-M39; shipped M40 except declined flag): - Ordinal categorical convenience flag. Declined M40 (owner sign-, 2026-07-01). proposed categorical = TRUE/FALSE argument ackwards() flipping cor (pearson→polychoric) ESEM estimator (ML/MLR→WLSMV) together. Rejected redundant: cor = "polychoric" already auto-selects WLSMV (estimator = NULL auto-rule, §9), categorical = TRUE pure synonym cor = "polychoric" — two-settings--one shortcut. add conflict surface (categorical = TRUE + cor = "pearson"?) §9 defaults change zero new capability. Discoverability handled docs layer instead (ordinal vignette already states cor = "polychoric" alone flips estimator; ordinal-detection cli warning names option runtime). R//DESCRIPTION/§9 change. - Ordinal correlation-comparison visualization. Done M40. two raw round(x$r[1:5,1:5], 2) matrix chunks vignettes/ackwards-ordinal.Rmd replaced one dodged bar chart ten N1–N5 lower-triangle item-pair correlations, fill = basis (Pearson vs polychoric), reshape code hidden. Viz-; ggplot2 already Suggests; package-code dependency change. (gt long-format Δ-table alternative set aside — vignette already carries two gt Δ-tables, chart adds variety directly addresses “matrices hard compare”.) - Forbes pruned-level axis-label styling. Done M40. fully-pruned level (every factor flagged) now gets italic autoplot() axis label normal (non-drop_pruned) render path, denoting status alongside existing grey node fill; partially-pruned levels keep plain label. Automatic (new argument). See §11.

title: Choosing an Engine: PCA, EFA, and ESEM
text: PCA extracts principal components — linear combinations observed variables capture maximum variance, including measurement error. Every item modeled communality 1.0: components account 100% item’s variance. true latent variable model; data reduction method. bass-ackwards context, PCA natural default. fast, always converges, produces eigenvalues can guide choice k. code: x_pca <- ackwards(bfi, k_max = 3, cor = "polychoric") x_pca #>  #> ── Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) ─────────────────────────────────────────── #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: polychoric #> n: 875 #> k (max): 3 #>  #> ── Levels ── #>  #> ✔ k = 1: 1 factor, 23.2% variance #> ✔ k = 2: 2 factors, 35.5% variance #> ✔ k = 3: 3 factors, 44.6% variance #>  #> ── Edges ── #>  #> 5 of 8 edges have |r| ≥ 0.3 #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Note: This is a series of linked solutions, not a fitted hierarchical model. #> Cross-level edges are descriptive score correlations. title: The Forbes Extension: Skip-Level Connections and Pruning
text: artifact factor one looks like copy factor another level rather genuine refinement — loading pattern closely resembles factor elsewhere hierarchy. Similarity measured Tucker’s congruence coefficient (φ): ϕ(Fa,Fb)=∑iλiaλib∑iλia2⋅∑iλib2\phi(F_a, F_b) = \frac{\sum_i \lambda_{ia}\lambda_{ib}} {\sqrt{\sum_i \lambda_{ia}^2 \cdot \sum_i \lambda_{ib}^2}} φ ranges −1 +1, values 0.95 conventionally read near-identical loading patterns regardless sign (Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge, 2006). Unlike "redundant", artifact mode never flags anything automatically. Forbes (2023) explicit identifying artifact requires researcher judgment — automating manufacture investigator degrees freedom — prune(x, "artifact") computes stores evidence weigh: Tucker’s φ every cross-level factor pair x$prune$phi, plus structural signals next section x$prune$structural. table read x$prune$phi. natural first cut non-adjacent pairs highest |φ| — deep factor whose loading pattern nearly duplicates factor two levels classic candidate: data strongest non-adjacent congruence |φ| = 0.99. Values near 1 mean deeper factor recycles earlier loading pattern; whether makes rotation artifact — faithfully persisting construct, redundancy view fact — judgment made substantive content items view, threshold package applies . two modes surface different fingerprints underlying question: "redundant": factor appears multiple levels near-identical score correlations — persists unchanged k increases. Auto-flagged (Forbes’s retention rule), score-correlation chain sharp, replicable criterion. "artifact": factor’s loading pattern closely resembles factor elsewhere hierarchy, structure looks -identified (next section). Reported — call .

what: The problem: which factor solution is right?
title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: Anyone applied factor analysis faces dilemma: many factors extract? Parallel analysis might say 5; scree plot might suggest 3; reviewer might insist 1. solution seems describe data differently, tempting treat competing answers question. Goldberg (2006) reframed problem: different solutions competing — complementary. 1-factor solution captures broadest shared variance data, dimension along variables correlate. 5-factor solution captures narrower, specific dimensions. two levels resolution describe underlying structure different vantage points, just satellite image street map describe city. bass-ackwards method makes relationship explicit. fits factor models every level 1 k, computes correlations factor scores across adjacent levels. correlations show : narrow factor inherits broad factor — lineage dimension move coarse fine resolution. factor splits — single broad factor correlates strongly two narrower factors fragmenting sub-dimensions. factor stable — factor correlates ~1.0 counterpart adjacent level essentially construct appearing two resolutions. result hierarchy map — visual numerical account factor structure data builds broad narrow. Note: descriptive, data-driven hierarchy, confirmatory hierarchical model like Schmid–Leiman higher-order SEM. -level correlations score correlations (algebraic equivalents), model parameters.

Within level, ackwards() rotates factors orthogonally using varimax, merely cosmetic default. Orthogonality gives -level score correlations closed form: orthonormal rotation satisfies T′ = T⁻¹, exactly makes W′RW edge algebra exact rather approximation. Varimax orthogonal rotation Goldberg (2006) used, rotation “CF-VARIMAX” reported Mplus-based papers Kim & Eaton (2015) — CF(κ = 1/p) varimax — two labels competing choices. what: Why Pearson underestimates for ordinal items
title: Ordinal Data: Polychoric Correlations and WLSMV
text: continuous latent trait sliced small number ordered categories, observed correlation two items always lower correlation underlying latent variables. coarse rating scale, larger attenuation. 5-point scale, Pearson correlations can 10–20% lower true latent correlations; 2-point (binary) scale, underestimate can exceed 30%. matters bass-ackwards analysis : Loadings attenuated. Factors appear weaker . -level edges attenuated. hierarchy looks flatter — factors nearly perfectly correlated across levels may appear moderately correlated. number factors underestimated. Attenuation compresses eigenvalue spectrum, selection criteria (parallel analysis, MAP) may suggest fewer factors truly exist.

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: suggest_k() computes five criteria data. logic, typical behavior, practical limitations.

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: . Computes PC eigenvalues observed correlation matrix, simulates large number random correlation matrices dimensions. Retains components whose observed eigenvalues exceed 95th percentile simulated distribution. Typical behavior. Tends overextract — often recommends components replicate independent samples, particularly items moderately correlated (usually personality clinical research). Treat recommendation upper bound. useful. Best paired engine = "pca" ackwards(), since operate PC eigenvalue basis. also provides fast, stable baseline across wide range data types. Limitation. 95th-percentile threshold convention, formal test. large samples, even trivial components can exceed threshold.

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: . logic PA-PC, applied common-factor eigenvalues rather PC eigenvalues. observed FA eigenvalues compared 95th percentile simulated FA eigenvalues. Typical behavior. conservative PA-PC. FA eigenvalues smaller (exclude unique variance), fewer exceed random-data threshold. PA-FA recommendations typically equal fall PA-PC. useful. model-consistent criterion engine = "efa" engine = "esem" ackwards(): analysis assumes common-factor model, comparing FA eigenvalues FA baseline better-matched test. Limitation. Like PA-PC, can underpowered small samples returns NA observed FA eigenvalue exceeds random threshold (sign data may support factor model ).

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?

Recommends k minimizes average — point components removed much shared variance possible. Typical behavior. Usually conservative — often recommends fewer factors PA. Simulation studies find performs well across range sample sizes factor structures, particularly true number factors small moderate. useful. reliable secondary check catches cases PA overextracts. title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: . Generates comparison datasets drawing marginal distributions observed items (preserving item’s shape without assuming multivariate normality). Computes eigenvalues comparison dataset applies sequential one-sided Wilcoxon test (default α = 0.30): factor retained adding significantly reduces RMSE relative previous level; procedure stops first non-significant improvement. recommended k last retained level — necessarily minimum RMSE curve. Typical behavior. Among accurate criteria simulation studies (Ruscio & Roche, 2012), particularly items non-normal distributions — common feature Likert-scale data. conservative PA-PC. useful. Ordinal skewed data normality assumptions underlying PA questionable. CD samples actual marginal distributions, implicitly captures item skewness discreteness. Limitation. Requires EFAtools package (install separately). Needs raw data matrix — run correlation matrix alone. resampling step stochastic, results can vary slightly across runs (use seed reproducibility). suggest_k() reports cd_available = FALSE EFAtools absent skips CD gracefully. note cor = "spearman": four criteria respect cor argument compute eigenvalues requested correlation matrix. title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis R ackwards implements Goldberg’s (2006) bass-ackwards method modern extensions mapping hierarchical structure multivariate data. core insight simple: instead asking many factors best describe data, ask solutions different levels resolution related one another. 1-factor solution captures broadest shared variance; 5-factor solution captures narrower, specific dimensions. Bass-ackwards analysis traces broad factors split narrow ones — narrow factors redundant re-combinations broader ones. package supports three extraction engines (PCA, EFA, ESEM), polychoric correlations ordinal data, Forbes’s (2023) extension reveals skip-level connections flags redundant artifactual factors.

text: data data frame numeric matrix observed variables (items columns, observations rows). Alternatively, pre-computed correlation matrix may supplied (square, symmetric, numeric matrix unit diagonal). correlation matrix supplied, engine must "pca" "efa" (ESEM requires raw data), missing cor arguments ignored. See Correlation-matrix input section . k_max Maximum number factors/components extract. Required; use suggest_k() uncertain. Sets depth hierarchy: levels 1 k_max extracted retained. (Note: suggest_k()'s k_max means something related distinct – max number factors/components evaluates recommending depth, depth ; see function's docs.) engine Extraction engine: "pca" (default), "efa", "esem". "esem" uses lavaan::efa() rotation-aware SEs per-level fit indices; recommended clinical/HiTOP workflow (Kim & Eaton, 2015; Forbush et al., 2024). Requires lavaan >= 0.6-13. cor Correlation basis: "pearson" (default), "spearman", "polychoric". PCA/EFA, "polychoric" computes polychoric correlation matrix via psych::polychoric() (requires psych). ESEM, triggers WLSMV estimation via lavaan. cli warning emitted ordinal-looking columns detected cor "polychoric". fm Factor extraction method passed psych::fa(); used engine = "efa". One "minres" (default, robust OLS), "ml" (maximum likelihood, gives chi-square fit converges less reliably deep levels), "pa" (principal axis). Ignored engine = "pca". estimator Estimation method ESEM engine. NULL (default) auto-selects: "WLSMV" cor = "polychoric", "ML" otherwise. Pass explicitly override: "ULSMV" (unweighted WLS), "MLR" (robust ML). cor = "polychoric" estimator = "ML"/"MLR" errors (lavaan support ML/MLR ordered indicators); "WLSMV"/ "ULSMV" continuous cor allowed (valid, atypical, continuous WLS/ADF estimator). Ignored PCA EFA engines. effective value (auto-selection) recorded x$meta$estimator (NA PCA/EFA). missing handle missing item responses. One : "pairwise" (default) – use available observations pairwise. PCA/EFA feeds stats::cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs"). ESEM WLSMV/ULSMV (ordinal), lavaan uses available.cases, computes polychoric thresholds correlations rows contribute pair – MCAR-valid uses full N. ESEM ML/MLR (continuous), lavaan uses listwise deletion internally edges computed pairwise correlation matrix; minor inconsistency documented $meta. warning emitted incomplete rows detected. "listwise" – complete rows used. Reduces data stats::complete.cases() fitting, correlation matrix, engine fit, edges consistent. n_obs result reflects reduced N. "fiml" – Full Information Maximum Likelihood. engine = "esem" (estimator = "ML"/"MLR"), passes missing = "fiml" lavaan::efa() derives edge correlations lavaan's FIML-estimated saturated model. engine = "pca"/"efa" (M38), correlation matrix estimated via psych::corFiml() fed usual W'RW algebra; requires cor = "pearson" (corFiml estimates multivariate-normal matrix) route announced via cli message. Errors WLSMV/ULSMV non-Pearson PCA/EFA basis. Note: FIML improves estimation missingness impute item responses; score materialisation (keep_scores = TRUE) still produces NA rows incomplete observations. See n_obs fit-index sample size PCA/EFA path. n_obs Number observations, (raw-data FIML path) string selecting N feeds fit indices. Correlation-matrix input: positive integer. Required engine = "efa" (psych::fa() needs N chi-square / RMSEA / TLI); optional "pca" (stored NA_integer_ omitted, disabling N-dependent fit statistics). Raw data: N normally taken nrow(data) numeric n_obs ignored (warning). exception missing = "fiml" engine = "pca"/"efa" (M38): psych::corFiml() estimates correlation matrix incomplete rows, n_obs may "total" (default – every row contributing FIML likelihood, matching FIML convention; Enders, 2010) "complete" (complete-case N, conservative lower bound). Point estimates (loadings, edges) depend choice; EFA fit indices , approximate two-step (FIML matrix normal-theory EFA) route regardless N (Zhang & Savalei, 2020). string n_obs accepted path. align_signs Logical; sign-align factors primary-parent lineage? Default TRUE. keep_scores Logical; store factor scores result? Default FALSE (recomputable via augment.ackwards()). TRUE, per-observation scores stored x$scores named list n x k_j matrices, one per level, standardized real score SDs (see augment.ackwards()). keep_fits Logical; store raw engine fit objects? Default FALSE. TRUE, per-level fit objects (psych lavaan) stored x$fits named list indexed level. seed Integer seed stochastic engines (used PCA captured reproducibility metadata). Default NULL. pairs level pairs compute edges : "adjacent" (default, classic Goldberg – consecutive levels) "" (Forbes extension – every pair levels, including skip-level correlations). prune() recomputes -pairs edges demand regardless setting, pruning require pairs = "" . cut_show Edges |r| >= cut_show flagged above_cut tidy() output. title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis — ackwards
text: engine = "pca" – original Goldberg (2006) method; fastest; never fails converge; Waller (2007) algebra exact components. rotation = "varimax" – T'=T^-1 property orthogonal rotation enables closed-form W'RW edge algebra keeps within-level factors uncorrelated cross-level edges reflect hierarchical signal. Matches Goldberg (2006), Kim & Eaton (2015), Forbush et al. (2024). Varimax supported rotation; oblique rotation confound -level signal method's core output. cor = "pearson" – silent basis switching. items look ordinal (<= 7 distinct integer values), cli warning suggest cor = "polychoric", available three engines. align_signs = TRUE – unaligned signs make output unreadable. Anchor: m1f1 oriented toward positive manifold; subsequent factor flipped edge primary parent positive. keep_scores = FALSE / keep_fits = FALSE – memory privacy. Scores O(n x Sigmak) often sensitive; raw engine fits can large. recomputable stored r matrix.

code: x <- ackwards(bfi25, k_max = 6, engine = "esem")  # expensive   x |> prune("redundant")                            # cheap, repeatable   x |> prune("redundant", redundancy_r = 0.95)        # no re-extraction

title: Flag redundant or artifactual factors (Forbes 2023 extension) — prune
text: x ackwards object. ... Reserved future methods/arguments. rules Character vector controlling auto-rules run. Default "none" (auto rule; combine manual pure manual pruning, call prune(x) arguments clear existing pruning). Options: "redundant" – identify chains factors connected primary-parent links |r| >= redundancy_r (optionally phi > redundancy_phi). Applies Forbes's (2023) retention rule: keep bottom node chain reaches level k_max (specific); keep top node otherwise. Flagged nodes get pruned = TRUE prune_reason = "redundant" x$prune$nodes. "artifact" (alias "artefact", normalized "artifact") – compute Tucker's congruence coefficient (phi) cross-level factor pairs store x$prune$phi, plus structural signals (few_items/orphan/split_merge) x$prune$structural. factors auto-flagged; artifact identification requires judgment (Forbes, 2023; Wicherts et al., 2016). manual Character vector factor labels (e.g. c("m4f3", "m4f4")) flag directly, addition instead auto rule. Standalone manual pruning supported: prune(x, manual = c("m4f3")). Unknown labels error. node already flagged auto rule keeps prune_reason; otherwise-unflagged manual nodes get prune_reason = "manual". redundancy_r Scalar (0, 1]. Adjacent primary-parent |r| threshold redundancy chains. Default 0.9 (Forbes, 2023). redundancy_phi Scalar (0, 1], NULL (default, auto), NA (explicit opt-). NULL: x$engine == "pca" – phi filter. Component scores determinate (exact linear functions data, factor-score indeterminacy), score correlation |r| true correlation components ; phi adds nothing |r| already capture. x$engine "efa" "esem" – automatically set 0.95 (Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge, 2006). Factor-score indeterminacy -PCA means |r|-alone liberal; conjunctive phi criterion conservative default. cli message announces resolved value. Pass NA disable phi filtering regardless engine. Pass numeric value override engine. min_items Minimum number items factor must primary loader (highest |loading|). Factors fewer min_items primary items flagged few_items = TRUE x$prune$structural. used rules includes "artifact". Default 3L – factor defined one two items -identified frequently extraction artifact rather replicable construct (classic "three-indicator rule"; Forbes, 2023, Fig. 2). orphan_r Threshold orphan structural signal. factor whose maximum adjacent-level |r| (immediately shallower deeper levels) falls orphan_r flagged orphan = TRUE x$prune$structural – connect neighbouring solutions replicate across hierarchy. used rules includes "artifact". Default 0.5 – moderate correlation; factor shares less quarter variance every neighbour structural outlier worth inspecting.

title: Simulated continuous bass-ackwards teaching example (16 items, known hierarchy) — sim16
text: Population model. Sigma = Lambda %*% Phi %*% t(Lambda) + Psi, oblique common-factor model 4 true group factors, sampled via Cholesky factorization (base R; MASS dependency): items load 0.75 true factor (cross-loadings). Factor correlations: 0.45 within metatrait (f1-f2, f3-f4), 0.15 metatraits. Uniquenesses 1 - communality (uniform 0.4375 symmetry design ). Ground-truth hierarchy (verified ackwards(engine = "efa")): k=1 recovers single general factor across 16 items; k=2 splits along metatrait line (i1-i8 vs. i9-i16); k=4 recovers 4 true group factors exactly; six suggest_k() recommendations (five criteria – VSS reports complexities 1 2) reach consensus k = 4. Idealized design. planted signal strong clean, six suggest_k() recommendations converge k = 4 – deliberately easy case, building intuition recovering known hierarchy looks like. Real data rarely agree cleanly: bfi25 criteria span k = 4–6. two datasets complementary teaching foils – sim16 "watch method recover structure planted," bfi25 "reason hierarchy criteria disagree." Present sim16's consensus ideal, norm. Deliberate overextraction artifact k=5. population exactly 4 factors, requesting 5th finds real dimension: EFA produces orphan factor zero primary-loading items. prune(x, "artifact") (default min_items = 3, orphan_r = 0.5), factor flagged few_items orphan. true (non- splitting) factors persist essentially unchanged k=3 onward, parent-child score correlations approach 1 flagged prune(x, "redundant") (|r| >= .9 , EFA auto-default, Tucker's phi > .95). textbook overextraction artifact, included Forbes/redundancy examples guaranteed finding teach (unlike bfi25, reliably trigger one). regenerate dataset, run source("data-raw/sim16.R") package root (set.seed(42)).

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis — suggest_k
text: Criteria available (controlled criteria argument): PA-PC ("pa_pc") – Horn (1965) parallel analysis PC eigenvalues. Compares observed eigenvalues random correlation matrices; suggests retaining components whose eigenvalues exceed 95th percentile chance. Tends overextract; treat upper bound. PA-FA ("pa_fa") – Horn (1965) parallel analysis using common-factor eigenvalues. conservative PA-PC better match EFA ESEM engines ackwards(). Shares one psych::fa.parallel() call PA-PC. MAP ("map") – Velicer (1976) Minimum Average Partial criterion. Finds k minimises average squared partial correlation remaining extracting k components. Usually conservative. Shares one psych::vss() call VSS. VSS ("vss") – Revelle & Rocklin (1979) Simple Structure fit complexities 1 2 (VSS-1 VSS-2). Finds k maximising fit simple loading structure. Shares one psych::vss() call MAP. CD ("cd") – Ruscio & Roche (2012) Comparison Data. Resamples observed item distributions generate comparison eigenvalue profiles; retains factors adding one longer improves RMSE beyond chance. Requires EFAtools package (install separately). Skipped informational message EFAtools absent correlation matrix supplied. PA (bases), MAP, VSS share correlation matrix ackwards(). CD operates raw data matrix directly (required resampling).

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis — suggest_k
text: k ackwards() maximum depth, claim exactly k factors exist. Users commonly set k one two levels consensus watch higher-level factors fragment – feature method, overextraction.

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis — suggest_k
text: PA-PC particular tends recommend factors replicate across independent samples, especially correlated items (Forbes, 2023). PA-FA CD conservative. Treat full set criteria range: true k likely somewhere middle.

title: Tidy an ackwards object into a long data frame — tidy.ackwards
text: x ackwards object. extract: "edges" (default) – one row per directed -level edge: , , level_from, level_to, r, is_primary, above_cut. "loadings" – one row per item x factor x level: level, factor, item, loading, se, ci_lower, ci_upper. se, ci_lower, ci_upper populated engine = "esem" (produces rotation-aware loading SEs); NA PCA EFA. confidence level controlled conf_level. "variance" – one row per factor x level: level, factor, proportion, cumulative. proportions total item variance 0-1 scale (multiply 100 percentage). "fit" – one row per fit statistic x level: level, statistic, value. PCA objects statistics eigenvalues; EFA objects chi, dof, p_value, RMSEA, TLI, BIC – chi likelihood-ratio chi-square (psych::fa()'s STATISTIC), chi, dof, p_value, RMSEA, TLI share one statistical framing (psych's residual-based empirical chi-square different statistic reported); ESEM chi, dof, p_value, CFI, TLI, RMSEA, SRMR, BIC. ESEM scaled-test estimator ("WLSMV"/"ULSMV" ordinal data, "MLR" continuous), whole row – chi/dof/ p_value CFI/TLI/RMSEA – reports lavaan's mean--variance-adjusted ("scaled") variant, every quantity shares one scaling. matters WLSMV/ULSMV: naive chi-square valid reference distribution (lavaan's summary() labels p-value "Unknown"), naive CFI/TLI badly optimistic ordinal data (Xia & Yang, 2019). "ML" scaled variant, reports naive values (correct ones ML). SRMR scaled variant reported -. BIC NA WLSMV/ULSMV (proper log-likelihood limited-information estimator) populated ML/MLR. Use format = "wide" one row per non-anchor level (k >= 2; saturated 1-factor anchor dropped, matching summary() autoplot(= "fit")), one column per statistic. Conventional fit cutoffs (Hu & Bentler 1999) shown reference lines autoplot(= "fit") inline summary(), returned pass/fail column – contested thresholds, report-, never gate anything (see functions' docs). format oriented EFA/ESEM model-fit statistics; PCA "statistics" per-component eigenvalues. "nodes" – Forbes-extension pruning annotations (requires prune != "none" object created). One row per factor across levels: id, level, pruned, prune_reason. Returns empty data frame columns pruning applied. "scores" – long-format per-observation factor scores (requires keep_scores = TRUE fit time use augment.ackwards() --fly computation). Columns: obs (row index), level, factor, score. primary_only = "edges" . TRUE, returns just factor's primary-parent edge (is_primary == TRUE) – lineage tree diagram draws solid arrows. Default FALSE (edges). Errors value . sort = "edges" . One "none" (default, natural order) "strength" (descending |r|). Ignored values . format = "fit" . One "long" (default, one row per statistic x level) "wide" (one row per level, one column per statistic). Errors values . conf_level = "loadings" . Confidence level loading intervals; default 0.95. intervals computed loading ± qnorm((1 + conf_level) / 2) * se NA engines carry SEs (PCA, EFA). Errors values . ... Ignored.

text: x ackwards object. level Integer vector selecting level(s) include. NULL (default) returns levels. cut Absolute-loading threshold. Items |loading| >= cut shown. Default 0.3, matching cut_show plotting default. n Maximum number items show per factor. NULL (default) shows items meeting cut. set, top-n items |loading| kept applying cut. sort Logical. TRUE (default), items within group ordered descending |loading|. Set FALSE keep original order (useful items meaningful sequence). One "factor" (default) "item". "factor" groups listing factor (salient items factor – "factor ?"). "item" inverts grouping list, item, factors loads – makes cross-loadings legible ("item go?"). n sort apply within whichever unit selects. show_labels Logical. TRUE (default) data carried variable-label attribute fit time (see ackwards()), items shown label (id); items without label fall back bare id. Set FALSE always show bare m{k}f{j}-style item ids.

ackwards 0.1.0
text: First public release. Licensed MIT.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Three engines available via engine argument ackwards(): PCA (engine = "pca") — original Goldberg (2006) bass-ackwards method. Fastest; never fails converge; exact W′RW algebra -level edges. EFA (engine = "efa") — exploratory factor analysis via psych::fa() tenBerge scoring weights closed-form algebra path. ESEM (engine = "esem") — EFA estimated SEM framework via lavaan::efa() WLSMV ordinal estimation, per-level fit indices (CFI, TLI, RMSEA, SRMR), rotation-aware standard errors. Required clinical/HiTOP workflow. engines use varimax (orthogonal) rotation — rotation compatible W′RW algebra. Oblique rotation unsupported design.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: suggest_k() runs five complementary retention criteria returns consensus range: PA-PC PA-FA — parallel analysis PC FA eigenvalue bases (psych::fa.parallel(fa = "")). MAP (Velicer 1976) VSS-1/VSS-2 (Revelle & Rocklin 1979) via psych::vss(). Comparison Data (Ruscio & Roche 2012) via EFAtools::CD() — skipped gracefully EFAtools installed. autoplot.suggest_k() produces ggplot2 diagnostic: four-panel 2×2 grid (scree/PA, MAP, VSS, CD RMSE) EFAtools installed, three-panel single-column layout (scree/PA, MAP, VSS) otherwise. returned object also carries cd_rmse (column means CD’s RMSE eigenvalue matrix) use CD panel.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: -level factor-score correlations computed via closed-form W′RW algebra linear engines (PCA, EFA, ESEM tenBerge weights) — scores need materialized. Standardization uses real score SDs sqrt(diag(W′RW)) rather assuming unit variance. materialized-scores fallback available cross-check test verifies two routes agree within tolerance.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Pass cor = "polychoric" use polychoric correlations basis PCA EFA. ESEM, lavaan’s polychoric matrix used edge computation (consistent fitted model). WLSMV estimator default cor = "polychoric" ESEM. ackwards() detects likely-ordinal columns (≤ 7 distinct integer values) emits suppressible warning cor = "pearson" set.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Three strategies via missing ackwards(): "pairwise" (default) — pairwise-complete correlations; warns NAs present. "listwise" — complete cases , consistent N across fit edges. "fiml" — Full Information ML via lavaan (ESEM ML/MLR ); edges derived FIML saturated model.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: pairs = "" computes -level correlations every level pair, revealing skip-level associations. prune = "redundant" flags chains adjacent |r| ≥ 0.9 (Forbes retention rule). prune = "artefact" computes Tucker’s φ researcher inspection; auto-flagging. autoplot() renders pruned views (drop_pruned = TRUE) skip-level arcs.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: ggplot2 layered diagram extensive customization: edge color sign, width/alpha |r|, solid/dashed strength; monochrome mode (mono); APA-formatted correlation labels (show_r, r_digits, r_label_size); custom node labels (node_labels); primary-mode (primary_only); level axis labels (show_level_labels); arrowhead toggle (show_arrows); fixed edge width (edge_linewidth); legend toggle (legend); Forbes-style pruned view (drop_pruned, compress_levels).

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: top_items(x, level, cut, n, sort) — lists salient items factor, filtered |loading| >= cut (default 0.3) sorted descending absolute loading. Arguments level, n, sort allow subsetting levels, capping items per factor, controlling order. Returns top_items S3 object grouped cli print method. label_template(x, style) — generates named-character-vector scaffold autoplot(x, node_labels = ...). Styles: "id" (default), "forbes" (level-letter + within-level index: "A1", "B1", "B2", …), "blank". Factor IDs canonical layout order; editable c(...) literal also printed copy-paste. See vignette("ackwards-interpret") end--end naming workflow: reading factor top_items(), hierarchy-aware naming (parent vs. child, blends), sign-alignment caveat, top_items() → label_template() → autoplot(node_labels = ...) round-trip.
